title: Heatwave leaves Khajuraho temples and streets deserted, businesses closed - The News Mill
full_text:
Khajuraho in Madhya Pradesh has experienced an intense heatwave that has significantly disrupted daily life and tourism in the UNESCO World Heritage site. Temperatures reached 46 to 47 degrees Celsius, rendering the Chandela-era temples, roads, and markets largely deserted.
Known for its historic temples and steady influx of domestic and international visitors, Khajuraho has seen a sharp decline in tourist numbers due to the extreme heat. Many traders have closed their shops, with restaurants and markets remaining largely unoccupied. Roads, typically crowded with tourists and locals, were observed to be mostly empty, with people venturing out only when necessary.
On Thursday, temperatures reached 46 degrees Celsius, intensifying concerns among residents and businesses. Locals described the atmosphere as similar to a lockdown, with the severe heat bringing normal activities to a near halt.
Local trader Rishi Tripathi told ANI, “The temperature has risen significantly. Business has completely come to a halt, and you can see deserted roads. Who will come in such heat? There is a major problem for businesses. The situation resembles a lockdown. Temples will function only if tourists come.”
The Khajuraho Municipal Council has arranged relief measures for commuters, including drinking water and shaded areas at public intersections. Chief Municipal Officer Basant Chaturvedi urged residents to avoid going out during the day, noting red alert conditions. He said, “Yesterday, the temperature touched 47 degrees Celsius. Roads are completely empty. We appeal to people not to step out during the day. The municipality has made arrangements by placing water pots at major intersections.”
Tourist Savita Jain compared the current situation to the COVID-19 lockdown, stating, “The temperature here is around 47 degrees Celsius. It feels like a COVID-like situation. It is becoming very difficult to step out of the house.”
The India Meteorological Department has issued Red and Orange alerts across several states due to temperatures exceeding 45 degrees Celsius. IMD scientist Naresh Kumar told ANI, “We anticipate that heatwave conditions will persist across parts of Northwest, Central, and most of East India for the next 6 to 7 days. We have issued an ‘Orange Alert’ for Punjab, Haryana, NCR-Delhi region, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Odisha.” He added, “We have also issued a ‘Red Alert’ for East Uttar Pradesh, East Madhya Pradesh and certain parts of Vidarbha for the next three days.”
